conditions.description: 200 bicycle storage spaces are proposed
conditions.rawText: The total of 560 parking spaces, as well as, 200 bicycle storage spaces are proposed.

conditions.description: Public art contribution
conditions.rawText: A public art contribution in accordance with the Percent for Public Art Program for a value not less than one percent of the gross construction cost for Phase 2 of the project prior to any above-grade building permit;

conditions.description: Provide and maintain irrigation system for trees
conditions.rawText: The owner shall provide and maintain an irrigation system, at the applicant's expense, for proposed trees within the public road allowances, including an automatic timer, designed to be water efficient by a Certified Landscape Irrigation Auditor (CLIA) and constructed with a back flow preventer to the satisfaction of the Executive Director, Technical Services Division, and requirements to maintain in good order and operation;

conditions.description: 1% of Phase II construction cost for public art
conditions.rawText: A public art contribution in accordance with the Percent for Public Art Program for a value not less than one percent of the gross construction cost for Phase II of the project prior to any above-grade building permit;

conditions.description: Maintain exterior building and landscape materials
conditions.rawText: The owner shall incorporate in the construction of the buildings and thereafter maintain exterior building and landscape materials to the satisfaction of the Chief Planner and Executive Director, City Planning Division;

conditions.description: A contribution of not less than one percent of the estimated gross construction costs for the above grade development on the lands known in 2010 as 14 Strachan Avenue and 39-51 East Liberty Street, including any density transferred to the lands known in 2010 as 19 Western Battery Road, which may be used for the City's Percent for Public Art Program or the acquisition of any additional parkland.
conditions.rawText: A contribution of not less than one percent of the estimated gross construction costs for the above grade development on the lands known in 2010 as 14 Strachan Avenue and 39-51 East Liberty Street, including any density transferred to the lands known in 2010 as 19 Western Battery Road, which may be used for: a. The City’s Percent for Public Art Program; or b. The acquisition of any additional parkland generally shown on Attachment 1 to the report from the Director, Community Planning, Toronto and East York District dated June 7, 2010.

conditions.description: The owner shall provide and maintain an irrigation system, at the applicant's expense, for proposed trees within the public road allowances, including an automatic timer, designed to be water efficient by a Certified Landscape Irrigation Auditor (CLIA) and constructed with a back flow preventer to the satisfaction of the Executive Director, Technical Services Division, and requirements to maintain in good order and operation.
conditions.rawText: The owner shall provide and maintain an irrigation system, at the applicant's expense, for proposed trees within the public road allowances, including an automatic timer, designed to be water efficient by a Certified Landscape Irrigation Auditor (CLIA) and constructed with a back flow preventer to the satisfaction of the Executive Director, Technical Services Division, and requirements to maintain in good order and operation.

conditions.description: Dedication and Above Base Park construction of new parkland to the satisfaction of the General Manager of Parks, Forestry & Recreation, generally as shown on Attachment 1.
conditions.rawText: Dedication and Above Base Park construction of new parkland to the satisfaction of the General Manager of Parks, Forestry & Recreation, generally as shown on Attachment 1 to the report from the Chief Planner and Executive Director, City Planning Division dated June 7, 2010.

conditions.description: Contribution of 1% of gross construction costs for the Public Art Program.
conditions.rawText: A contribution of not less than one percent of the estimated gross construction costs for the above grade development on the lands known in 2010 as 14 Strachan Avenue and 39-51 East Liberty Street, including any density transferred to the lands known in 2010 as 19 Western Battery Road, to be used for the City’s Percent for Public Art Program.

conditions.description: The developer must provide public art equal to 1% of the capital budget.
conditions.rawText: The amount dedicated to public art is one percent (1%) of the capital budget of the project consistent with City requirements.

conditions.description: The developer must complete the landscape plan including pathway and landscaping.
conditions.rawText: The landscape plans including the new pathway connection and associated landscaping will be secured through the site plan process.

conditions.description: Provide public art program at 1% of gross construction costs.
conditions.rawText: As per the previous agreement, the applicant will be required to develop and provide a public art program in the amount of 1% of the project's gross construction costs.

conditions.description: Conditions relating to relocation of Medallion and consultation with artist continue in amended agreement.
conditions.rawText: The conditions relating to the relocation of the Medallion and consultation with the artist will continue in the amended agreement.

conditions.description: Provide 20 secure on-site bicycle parking for staff; 80 bicycle parking spaces for visitors.
conditions.rawText: The proposal incorporates a number of Transportation Demand Management strategies for Phase 1 of the Aquarium including: … 20 secure on-site bicycle parking for staff; 80 bicycle parking spaces for visitors;

conditions.description: Provide showers and change rooms for staff.
conditions.rawText: The proposal incorporates a number of Transportation Demand Management strategies for Phase 1 of the Aquarium including: showers and change rooms for staff,

conditions.description: Architectural feature at southwest corner of building
conditions.rawText: an architectural feature integrated into the southwest corner of the building on the lot (intersection of Bathurst Street and Front Street), to the satisfaction of the Chief Planner and Executive Director, City Planning, in consultation with the Ward Councillor and the submission, prior to issuance of the first above grade building permit, of a letter of credit in an amount and form satisfactory to the Chief Planner and Executive Director to secure the estimated cost of design, construction and installation of the architectural feature;

conditions.description: Secure location and type of bicycle parking spaces in the zoning by-law.
conditions.rawText: The location of the bicycle parking spaces, as well as the type of parking spaces (horizontal, vertical or stacked) will be secured in the zoning by-law.

conditions.description: An architectural feature on the southeast corner of Front and Bathurst Streets, incorporated into the building design, secured with a letter of credit for full cost posted prior to first above-grade permit.
conditions.rawText: An architectural feature to be located on the southeast corner of the intersection at Front and Bathurst Streets, incorporated into the design of the building, will be secured, to the satisfaction of the Ward Councillor and the Chief Planner. A letter of credit for the full cost of this feature will be posted by the applicant prior to the first above grade permit on the site.

conditions.description: The owner must provide a $500,000 cash contribution for public art as per the Section 37 agreement.
conditions.rawText: The Section 37 agreement dated August 25, 2014 was signed between the 'Owner' and the 'City'. The Public Art contribution under section 3. Section 37 Obligations states that 'Five hundred Thousand dollars (Canadian) ($500,000.00 CDN) (hereinafter referred to as the "Art Contribution") for public art in accordance with the City of Toronto's Percent for Public Art Program.

conditions.description: Any redevelopment of the lands at 2 Tecumseth Street and 28 Bathurst Street will be required, as a condition of approval, through public art contribution, or other means in consultation with the Manager of Heritage Preservation Services, to provide reference to the built, natural and/or contextual heritage of the South Niagara Area and surroundings.
conditions.rawText: Any redevelopment of the lands at 2 Tecumseth Street and 28 Bathurst Street will be required, as a condition of approval, through public art contribution, or other means in consultation with the Manager of Heritage Preservation Services, to provide reference to the built, natural and/or contextual heritage of the South Niagara Area and surroundings.

conditions.description: The City will explore opportunities to retain the original paving within the proposed new woonerf/public street located on the west end of the Wellington Destructor site where possible.
conditions.rawText: The City will explore opportunities to retain the original paving within the proposed new woonerf/public street located on the west end of the Wellington Destructor site where possible.

conditions.description: Landscaping, tree planting, and streetscape requirements to be secured at Site Plan Control
conditions.rawText: New landscaping and amenity areas at the west and east side of the building and new tree plantings would provide an improved landscape area and outdoor amenity space. Further landscaping details will be secured at the Site Plan Control stage.

conditions.description: Provision of 38 bicycle parking spaces (including 5 visitor spots)
conditions.rawText: The proposed provision of zero vehicular parking spaces and 38 bicycle parking spaces is expected to provide sufficient capacity for the residents of the proposed development.

conditions.description: Obtain necessary permits and submit a satisfactory replanting plan before removing protected trees.
conditions.rawText: The applicant will be required to obtain the necessary permits and submit a satisfactory replanting plan prior to the removal of any protected trees.

conditions.description: 10% three bedroom unit count to be provided.
conditions.rawText: 10% three bedroom unit count to be provided.

conditions.description: Provide a detailed Landscape Plan.
conditions.rawText: Provide a detailed Landscape Plan for the subject property, satisfactory to the Senior Manager, Heritage Preservation Services.

conditions.description: A cash contribution of $1,650,000.00 for above base streetscape improvements on Mercer Street between John Street and Blue Jays Way.
conditions.rawText: A cash contribution of $1,650,000.00 for above base streetscape improvements on Mercer Street between John Street and Blue Jays Way, to the satisfaction of the Chief Planner and Executive Director, City Planning Division, in consultation with the Ward Councillor.

conditions.description: Sidewalk and front yard landscaping
conditions.rawText: A 2.1 metre sidewalk is proposed, in addition to 3.4 metres of front yard landscaping which will be secured through the site plan process.

conditions.description: The owner must make a contribution to Public Art in the amount of $500,000.
conditions.rawText: Schedule "G" Public Art Contribution of the agreement under 3. states that "The Owner agrees to make a contribution to Public Art in the amount of $500,000"

conditions.description: The Public Art process must be undertaken in consultation with OCAD U, the Grange Community Association, and the Ward Councillor.
conditions.rawText: The Section 37 agreement under Schedule "G" under 4. states that "The Owner acknowledges and agrees that the Public Art process will be undertaken in consultation with OCAD U, the Grange Community Association, and the Ward Councillor to the satisfaction of the Chief Planner."

conditions.description: The Public Art Plan includes a mentoring bursary to OCAD U to support student-interns.
conditions.rawText: The Public Art Plan also includes a mentoring bursary to OCAD U to support student-interns who will be involved through the public art project with responsibilities laid out by OCAD U faculty in consultation with the Public Art Consultant and the Owner.

conditions.description: $500,000 for public art on the lands, in consultation with OCAD University
conditions.rawText: 2. $500,000 for public art on the lands, in consultation with OCAD University, to be secured by a letter of credit in the City's standard form. The owner shall submit a Public Art Plan that is in accordance with the City's Percent for Public Art Guidelines to the satisfaction of the Chief Planner and Executive Director, City Planning Division, and details of the public art process to the satisfaction of City Council;

conditions.description: Construct and maintain at least 500 square metres at grade as privately-owned publicly accessible open space (POPS) extending between St. Patrick and Simcoe Streets.
conditions.rawText: construct and maintain an area of not less than 500 square metres at grade for use by the general public as a privately-owned publicly accessible open space (POPS), extending through the site between St. Patrick Street and Simcoe Street in a location generally identified in the Zoning By-law Amendments, with the specific configuration and design of the POPS to be determined in the context of Site Plan Approval for the development to the satisfaction of the Acting Director, Community Planning, Toronto and East York District, in consultation with the Ward Councillor;

conditions.description: Construct and convey a 1,000 sq.m. on-site parkland dedication at the southwest corner of the site.
conditions.rawText: The owner shall construct and convey to the City, an on-site parkland dedication having a minimum size of 1,000 square metres, situated at the southwest corner of the site, as shown generally on the Site Plan drawing A202, prepared by Hariri Pontarini Architects, dated September 1, 2017, revision 3 dated September 20, 2017, to the satisfaction of the General Manager, Parks, Forestry and Recreation, and in accordance with the terms and conditions of parkland conveyance and construction as set out in the draft Zoning By-law Amendments in Attachments 9 and 10 to this report.

conditions.description: Financial contribution of $500,000 for public art on the lands, to be secured by letter of credit, with submission of a Public Art Plan.
conditions.rawText: B. $500,000 for public art on the lands, in consultation with OCAD University, to be secured by a letter of credit in the City's standard form. The owner shall submit a Public Art Plan that is in accordance with the City's Percent for Public Art Guidelines to the satisfaction of the Chief Planner and Executive Director, City Planning Division, and details of the public art process to the satisfaction of City Council;

conditions.description: Construct and maintain a minimum 500 sq m privately-owned publicly accessible open space at grade, and convey a public access easement.
conditions.rawText: Prior to the earlier of one (1) year following any non-residential or residential use or occupancy, including interim occupancy pursuant to the Condominium Act, 1998, and registration of a condominium on the lot, except as otherwise agreed by the Chief Planner and Executive Director, City Planning Division, due to unforeseen delays (eg. weather), the owner shall: A. Construct and maintain an area of not less than 500 square metres at grade for use by the general public as privately-owned publicly accessible open space extending diagonally through the site and adjacent to St. Patrick Street and Simcoe Street in a location generally as shown on Map 3, with the specific configuration and design of the privately-owned publicly accessible open space to be determined in the context of site plan approval to the satisfaction of the Chief Planner and Executive Director, City Planning Division, in consultation with the Ward Councillor; and B. Prepare all documents and convey to the satisfaction of the Chief Planner and Executive Director, City Planning Division, and the City Solicitor, free and clear of encumbrances and for nominal consideration, a public access easement, including support rights, in perpetuity in favour of the City over the privately-owned publicly accessible open space, on terms set out in the

conditions.description: $500,000 for public art on the lands, in consultation with OCAD University, with a Public Art Plan.
conditions.rawText: $500,000 for public art on the lands, in consultation with OCAD University, to be secured by a letter of credit in the City's standard form. The owner shall submit a Public Art Plan that is in accordance with the City's Percent for Public Art Guidelines to the satisfaction of the Chief Planner and Executive Director, City Planning Division, and details of the public art process to the satisfaction of City Council; and

conditions.description: Construction and maintenance of a 500 square metre publicly accessible open space at grade.
conditions.rawText: Construct and maintain an area of not less than 500 square metres at grade for use by the general public as privately-owned publicly accessible open space extending diagonally through the site and adjacent to St. Patrick Street and Simcoe Street in a location generally as shown on Diagram 4, with the specific configuration and design of the privately-owned publicly accessible open space to be determined in the context of site plan approval to the satisfaction of the Chief Planner and Executive Director, City Planning Division, in consultation with the Ward Councillor; and

conditions.description: public art
conditions.rawText: public art

conditions.description: streetscape improvements on the public boulevard not abutting the site
conditions.rawText: streetscape improvements on the public boulevard not abutting the site

conditions.description: Submit written confirmation committing to heritage interpretation requirements and implementation of Final Heritage Interpretation Plan
conditions.rawText: Before introducing the necessary Bills for the Zoning By-law Amendments to City Council for enactment, City Council require Infrastructure Ontario (IO) and Ministry of the Attorney General to submit written confirmation to the Chief Planner and Executive Director, City Planning and Senior Manager, Heritage Preservation Services that IO will commit to leading and assume full responsibility for the heritage interpretation requirements as outlined in the Site Plan Pre-approval conditions contained in the May 20, 2016 comment letter from Heritage Preservation Services and maintain all responsibility for the development and implementation of the Final Heritage Interpretation Plan, including project management and planning, curation of artifacts and associated materials and long term maintenance.

conditions.description: Provide a Heritage Interpretation Plan (HIP) as a condition of site plan approval
conditions.rawText: HPS requires a comprehensive interpretive programme for the project and will be requiring a Heritage Interpretation Plan (HIP) as a condition of the site plan approval for the new courthouse building.

conditions.description: Lay sod and maintain site free of garbage and weeds
conditions.rawText: That sod be laid on the site and that the site be maintained free of garbage and weeds, in accordance with the Municipal Code Chapter 629-10, paragraph B and 629-11; and

conditions.description: Obtain necessary permits and submit a satisfactory replanting plan before removing protected trees.
conditions.rawText: The applicant will be required to obtain the necessary permits and submit a satisfactory replanting plan prior to the removal of any protected trees.

conditions.description: Owner must obtain permits and submit a replanting plan before removing protected trees.
conditions.rawText: The owner will be required to obtain necessary permits and submit a satisfactory replanting plan prior to the removal of any protected trees.

conditions.description: Lay sod on the site and maintain the site free of garbage and weeds in accordance with Municipal Code Chapter 629-10, paragraph B and 629-11.
conditions.rawText: That sod be laid on the site and that the site be maintained free of garbage and weeds, in accordance with the Municipal Code Chapter 629-10, paragraph B and 629-11;

conditions.description: The owner shall provide tenants of replacement rental dwelling units with access to all bicycle parking, car parking, visitor parking and storage lockers on the same terms and conditions as any other building resident;
conditions.rawText: The owner shall provide tenants of replacement rental dwelling units with access to all bicycle parking, car parking, visitor parking and storage lockers on the same terms and conditions as any other building resident;

conditions.description: Prior to the issuance of the first above-grade building permit, a cash contribution of $1,060,000 towards streetscape and/or public realm improvements in the vicinity of the site...
conditions.rawText: Prior to the issuance of the first above-grade building permit, a cash contribution of $1,060,000 towards streetscape and/or public realm improvements in the vicinity of the site, to the satisfaction of the Chief Planner and Executive Director, City Planning Division, in consultation with the Ward Councillor;

conditions.description: Prior to the issuance of any above grade building permits, the owner shall submit landscape plans to the satisfaction of the Chief Planner and Executive Director, City Planning, and the Supervisor, Tree Protection & Plan Review, Urban Forestry.
conditions.rawText: Prior to the issuance of any above grade building permits, the owner shall submit landscape plans to the satisfaction of the Chief Planner and Executive Director, City Planning, and the Supervisor, Tree Protection & Plan Review, Urban Forestry.

conditions.description: Provide privacy screens and/or planting beds on the patios and balconies facing the west to mitigate overlook, to be secured through the Site Plan application process.
conditions.rawText: These concerns will be mitigated by providing privacy screens and/or planting beds on the patios and balconies facing the west. The details of these screens and planting beds will be secured through the Site Plan application process.

conditions.description: Zoning by-law will secure cycling infrastructure performance measures (includes bike parking).
conditions.rawText: The site specific Zoning By-law will secure performance measures for the following Tier 1 development features: cycling infrastructure and bird friendly design.

conditions.description: Prior to the issuance of the first above-grade building permit, a cash contribution of $1,060,000 towards streetscape and/or public realm improvements in the vicinity of the site, to the satisfaction of the Chief Planner and Executive Director, City Planning Division, in consultation with the Ward Councillor.
conditions.rawText: Prior to the issuance of the first above-grade building permit, a cash contribution of $1,060,000 towards streetscape and/or public realm improvements in the vicinity of the site, to the satisfaction of the Chief Planner and Executive Director, City Planning Division, in consultation with the Ward Councillor.

conditions.description: Cash contribution towards streetscape and/or public realm improvements.
conditions.rawText: Prior to the issuance of the first above-grade building permit, a cash contribution of $1,060,000 towards streetscape and/or public realm improvements in the vicinity of the site, to the satisfaction of the Chief Planner and Executive Director, City Planning Division, in consultation with the Ward Councillor;

conditions.description: Tenants of affordable units provided access to long-term and short-term bicycle parking on same basis as other units.
conditions.rawText: tenants of the Affordable Rental Housing Units will be provided with access to long-term and short-term bicycle parking, and, where provided, visitor vehicular parking, in accordance with the Zoning By-law and on the same basis as other units within the development

conditions.description: Conserve existing trees and submit tree replacement plan if removal is needed.
conditions.rawText: the owner has worked with City Planning and Urban Forestry staff to conserve as many of the five existing City trees as possible along the Spadina Avenue frontage. For any trees that do require removal to facilitate construction, the owner has submitted an application to remove City trees together with a Tree Planting Replacement Plan, to the satisfaction of Urban Forestry;

conditions.description: Applicant must provide, install, and maintain public art (mural artwork) on hoarding board elevations with spotlighting.
conditions.rawText: City Council direct the applicant to provide, install, and maintain public art, including mural artwork, onto every elevation of the hoarding board with adequate spotlighting for night-time illumination, at their sole cost, to the satisfaction of the Ward Councillor.

conditions.description: Short-term bicycle parking to be provided on Richmond Street West
conditions.rawText: short-term bicycle parking will be provided on Richmond Street West

conditions.description: Existing six City trees on Bathurst Street to be maintained
conditions.rawText: the six existing City trees on Bathurst Street will be maintained

conditions.description: Plant four new street trees on Richmond Street West right-of-way
conditions.rawText: four new street trees will be planted on the Richmond Street West right-of-way

conditions.description: Local infrastructure improvements and public art with additional payment
conditions.rawText: Local Infrastructure Improvements and Public Art in accordance with the terms in the existing Section 37 agreements, with any such additional payment associated with the proposed increase to the Phase 1 development to be made by the owner prior to the issuance of the first above-grade building permit for the expanded proposal;

conditions.description: Increased provision of short-term bicycle parking for the additional 114 residential units, at a rate of 0.2 short term spaces per unit.
conditions.rawText: Increased provision of short-term bicycle parking for the additional 114 residential units, at a rate of 0.2 short term spaces per unit

conditions.description: On-site at-grade landscaping using 50% native, drought-tolerant species.
conditions.rawText: on-site at-grade landscaping using 50% native, drought-tolerant species (EC 2.2)

conditions.description: Green and cool paving for 75% of the site's non-roof hardscape.
conditions.rawText: green and cool paving for 75% of the site's non-roof hardscape (EC 2.1)

conditions.description: Public art contribution of one percent of gross construction costs
conditions.rawText: a public art contribution of one percent of gross construction costs.

conditions.description: The developer must pay $1,000,000 for public art and submit a Public Art Plan.
conditions.rawText: a cash contribution of $1,000,000.00 for public art on the lands to be provided prior to the issuance of the first above-grade building permit for Phase 2 of the development on the lands, to be secured by a letter of credit in the City's standard form, to be indexed upwardly from the date City Council adopted Zoning By-law 1014-2022 to the date the payment is made. The owner shall submit a Public Art Plan, prior to site plan approval for Phase 2 of the development on the lands, that is in accordance with the City's Percent for Public Art Guidelines to the satisfaction of the Executive Director, Development Review and details of the public art process to the satisfaction of City Council.

conditions.description: The developer must submit a Public Realm and Landscape Master Plan for all phases.
conditions.rawText: the owner shall submit a Public Realm and Landscape Master Plan for all phases at the time of Site Plan Approval for Phase 1 of the development, to the satisfaction of the Executive Director, Development Review;

conditions.description: Public art secured through amend timing for letter of credit provision
conditions.rawText: The Section 37 Agreement proposes to amend the timing for the provision of the letter of credit to be provided prior to the issuance of the first above-grade building permit for Phase 2 of the development.

conditions.description: Raised cycle track on Blue Jays Way frontage to be secured through Phase 1 Site Plan
conditions.rawText: The application proposes a raised cycle track on the Blue Jays Way frontage of the site. The cycle track will be secured through the Site Plan application for Phase 1 of the development.

conditions.description: Replacement planting required for removed trees
conditions.rawText: Forestry staff have advised that applications will be required for the proposed removal of the street trees and trees in Isabella Valancy Crawford Park and that replacement planting is required.

conditions.description: Minimum 0.9 long-term bicycle parking spaces per dwelling unit.
conditions.rawText: a minimum of 0.9 long-term bicycle parking spaces per dwelling unit

conditions.description: Minimum 0.2 short-term bicycle parking spaces per dwelling unit.
conditions.rawText: a minimum of 0.2 short-term bicycle parking spaces per dwelling unit

conditions.description: Minimum 3 bicycle parking spaces plus 0.4 per 100 sq m of non-residential GFA.
conditions.rawText: a minimum of 3 bicycle parking spaces plus 0.4 bicycle parking spaces for every 100 square metres of non-residential gross floor area

conditions.description: Universal shower and change facilities must be provided based on the number of required long-term bicycle parking spaces.
conditions.rawText: universal shower and change facilities shall be provided on the same lot at the following rates: A. none if less than 5 required long-term bicycle parking spaces; B. 1 for 5 to 60 required long-term bicycle parking spaces; C. 2 for 61 to 120 required long-term bicycle parking spaces; D. 3 for 121 to 180 required long-term bicycle parking spaces; E. 4 for more than 180 required long-term bicycle parking spaces

conditions.description: Cash contribution of $1,000,000 for public art
conditions.rawText: Prior to the issuance of the first above-grade building permit, unless otherwise specified, the owner shall make a contribution in the amount of $1,250,000 to be allocated toward the following capital improvements at the discretion of the Executive Director, Development Review and the Chief Planner and Executive Director, City Planning in consultation with the Ward Councillor. (b) a cash contribution of $1,000,000 for public art on the lands to be provided prior to the issuance of the first above-grade building permit for the Phase 2 portion of the development, to be secured by a letter of credit in the standard form, to be indexed upwardly from the date City Council adopts this Zoning By-law Amendment to the date the payment is made.

conditions.description: Submission of a public art plan
conditions.rawText: Prior to the issuance of the first above-grade building permit, for the Phase 2 portion of the development the owner shall submit a public art plan detailing the design and construction of the public art installation in accordance with the City's Percent for Public Art Guidelines to the satisfaction of the Executive Director, Development Review and details of the public art process to the satisfaction of City Council.

conditions.description: Submit Public Realm and Landscape Master Plan at Site Plan Approval for Phase 1
conditions.rawText: The owner shall submit a Public Realm and Landscape Master Plan for the site at the time of Site Plan Approval for Phase 1 of the development, to the satisfaction of the Executive Director, Development Review;
